content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client clearly shifts from her opening posture to a genuinely new state by turn 15. She starts in performative gallows humor, intellectualizing the problem and trying to hand the coach a better question ('why do I always end up with people who just cannot show up?', 'maybe the question is less about what happens in my body and more about why they always stop'). By turn 13, the defenses thin: she names that 'I'm not sure I want to look at it directly' and articulates the configuration she's organized around ('I know how to be the one trying... I can't actually picture just being received'). In turn 15, a somatic shift lands — tightness in chest, an involuntary raw laugh, the impulse to cry, and the genuinely exposed question 'Why would that make me want to cry?' This is exactly the breakthrough condition: she touches the abyss-like feeling underneath being adored openly, and it is felt, not just named. The 'huh' moment and the change from quick-witted deflection to quiet, exposed checking-in are textbook Joe-style markers of shift visible in client turns.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 3, 7, 9, 11, 13, 15],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opening: gallows humor, victim frame, intellectualized problem-statement."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Deflection into partner-picking advice request — surface presentation intact."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Quick smile 'too quick,' returns to the pattern question; defenses still up."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Hands the coach a substitute question; but a crack appears — 'it goes blank,' 'hard to imagine.'"},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "First acknowledgment of redirection; 'trying to picture a color I haven't seen' — new language for the avoided territory."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Hears her own 'totally fine' as defensive; goes still — a shift from fast expressive energy."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the configuration as something she knows how to do; touches the receiving-stance she can't picture. Tries to close with attachment-theory label."},
    {"turn": 15, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Somatic landing: tight chest, raw laugh, wants to cry, genuine exposed confusion. Breakthrough condition met — she feels the abyss under being adored."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["looking-for-whats-wrong", "defensiveness-as-identity-signal", "feeling-the-unfelt-emotion", "if-i-were-inherently-good", "what-are-you-defending"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["deconstruct-the-victim-to-release-anger", "trauma-mapping"],
  "notes": "Conversation ends right at the threshold of grief/abyss work — the breakthrough moment is live and unresolved, but the shift itself is unambiguous in client text."
}
